Paper Title/Study: The effect of iron-fortified complementary food and intermittent preventive treatment of malaria on anaemia in 12- to 36-month-old children: a cluster-randomised controlled trial
Authors: Dominik Glinz, Richard F. Hurrell, Mamadou Ouattara, Michael B. Zimmermann, Gary M. Brittenham, Lukas G. Adiossan, Aurélie A. Righetti, Burkhardt Seifert, Victorine G. Diakité, Jürg Utzinger, Eliézer K. N’Goran, Rita Wegmüller
Food Component: Iron-fortified complementary food (NaFeEDTA + ferrous fumarate or ferric pyrophosphate)
Medical Treatment: Intermittent preventive treatment (IPT) of malaria (sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine and amodiaquine)
Biomarkers: haemoglobin (Hb) concentration, plasma ferritin (PF), C-reactive protein (CRP), α-1-acid-glycoprotein (AGP)
Study Population: 629 children aged 12–36 months in south-central Côte d’Ivoire
Outcome / Finding: Haemoglobin concentration, anaemia prevalence, iron deficiency prevalence, malaria prevalence
Contribution: Problem: To assess whether iron fortification combined with IPT of malaria is an efficacious strategy for reducing anaemia in 12- to 36-month-old children in a malaria-endemic area.
Contribution: Result: IPT alone modestly decreased anaemia, but neither IPT nor iron-fortified CF significantly improved Hb concentration after 9 months. Iron-fortified CF markedly decreased iron deficiency but did not reduce anaemia prevalence.

Paper Title/Study: Cochrane review on iron fortification in malaria-endemic regions
Contribution: Problem: Effect of iron fortification on anaemia in malaria-endemic regions.
Contribution: Result: Reported a positive effect of iron fortification at point of consumption with micronutrient powders on anaemia prevalence in malaria-endemic regions.

Paper Title/Study: Tanzanian infant iron supplementation and IPT study
Medical Treatment: Iron supplementation and IPT of malaria
Contribution: Problem: Effect of iron supplementation combined with IPT of malaria on anaemia.
Contribution: Result: Found no additional effect on anaemia when iron supplementation was combined with IPT in 12- to 16-week-old Tanzanian infants.
